status: proposed
date: 2026-04-15
decision-makers: [lgrula]
---

# 0003 — Validate Generated Files Immediately at Write Time During Scaffolding

## Context and Problem Statement

When `nit:init` generates config and registry JSON files, each file must conform to its
JSON Schema. The question is when validation runs: immediately after each file is written
(fail-fast), or after all files are written (batch). This decision also applies to any
future skill that generates `.nit/` state files (e.g., `nit:phases`, `nit:tasks`).

## Decision Drivers

- ADR-0002 established ajv-based `nit validate` as the canonical validation mechanism
- Invalid state files cause silent failures at supervisor startup, far from the point of creation
- A bug in a skill's hardcoded defaults should surface immediately, not in a later phase
- Users should receive precise, actionable errors rather than generic "validation failed" messages

## Considered Options

- Option 1: Validate each file immediately after writing (fail-fast per-file)
- Option 2: Validate all files at the end of scaffolding (batch)
- Option 3: Validate only at supervisor startup (deferred)

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: "Option 1 — Validate each file immediately after writing", because it
provides the tightest feedback loop, surfaces the exact file and schema error before any
subsequent files are written, and prevents partially-valid state from being silently
committed to disk.

### Consequences

- Good, because errors pinpoint exactly which file and which schema field failed
- Good, because no downstream skill ever reads invalid state from a fresh init
- Good, because the pattern is composable — any future file-generating skill follows the same rule
- Bad, because init halts mid-scaffold if a validation fails, leaving a partial `.nit/` tree; the
  skill must communicate that the user should delete `.nit/` and re-run after fixing the issue

### Confirmation

- Each file generated by `nit:init` is followed by a `npx nit validate --schema <type> <file>` call
- If the validate call exits non-zero, the skill halts and surfaces the error output
- No subsequent files are written after a validation failure
- The REVIEW.md for TASK-011 verifies this order of operations in the implementation

## Pros and Cons of the Options

### Option 1 — Validate each file immediately after writing

- Good, because pinpoints the exact file that failed
- Good, because prevents writing further files on top of a bad state
- Good, because establishes a clear pattern for all future file-generating skills
- Bad, because a failure leaves a partial `.nit/` tree that must be cleaned up

### Option 2 — Validate all files at the end of scaffolding

- Good, because the directory tree is always complete before any error is reported
- Good, because easier to diagnose all problems in one pass
- Bad, because multiple files may be invalid but errors are harder to associate with their causes
- Bad, because all files are on disk even though some are invalid

### Option 3 — Validate only at supervisor startup

- Good, because init is simpler and always "succeeds"
- Bad, because errors are reported far from their origin (init bug surfaces during first task run)
- Bad, because the user may have made changes between init and first run, obscuring the root cause

## More Information

- Related ADR: .nit/adr/0002-json-schema-2020-12-with-ajv-library.md
- Related design: .nit/phases/PHASE-2/tasks/TASK-011/DESIGN.md
